Title: OsCYP706C2 diverts rice strigolactone biosynthesis to a noncanonical pathway branch

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Oryza sativa; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch point, upstream/midstream step after carlactone
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms pathway intermediate and diverts flux toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes carlactone to 4-oxo-19-OH-carlactone, an intermediate en route to 4-oxo-MeCLA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-switch enzyme for noncanonical strigolactone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes biosynthesis and exudation of the noncanonical strigolactone 4-oxo-MeCLA; mutation abolishes 4-oxo-MeCLA, increases flux to canonical 4DO/orobanchol, alters root development, and delays AM colonization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cross-species and rice coexpression analyses identified OsCYP706C2 as a strigolactone biosynthetic candidate in a Poaceae gene cluster. 2. Heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana showed OsCYP706C2 consumes carlactone, not MeCLA, and produces 4-oxo-19-OH-carlactone. 3. CRISPR oscyp706c2 mutants lacked 4-oxo-MeCLA in root exudates while canonical 4DO and orobanchol remained. 4. Mutants showed reduced crown root number/root fresh weight and delayed early arbuscular mycorrhizal colonization.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cross-species coexpression analysis; mutual-rank RNA-seq coexpression; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; yeast microsome assay; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; root exudate analysis; AM colonization assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-expressed; induced by phosphate deficiency and down-regulated after phosphate resupply; expression profile highly similar to Os900
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In rice roots under low phosphate; transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves; heterologously expressed in yeast microsomes
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphate deficiency induces expression and strigolactone production; hydroponic normal Pi vs low Pi; AM fungal inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rhizosphere signaling molecule; promotes early arbuscular mycorrhizal symbiosis; can stimulate Striga germination at relatively high concentration
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes reduced crown root number, reduced root fresh weight under low Pi, delayed AM colonization; no tillering phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MeCLA was tested but is not the preferred substrate. The paper assumes, by analogy to maize ZmCYP706C37, that C19 hydroxylation may occur first. Mutation also increased an unidentified 4-oxo-MeCLA isomer.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful to manipulate rice strigolactone blend and rhizosphere signaling without altering tillering; may allow tuning AM symbiosis versus canonical SL output, but potential root-development tradeoffs should be monitored
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq coexpression using GEO GSE74856 and Rice Expression Database

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes 4-oxo-19-OH-carlactone to 4-oxo-carlactonoic acid in the noncanonical branch; also catalyzes canonical SL formation from carlactone as previously reported
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key shared enzyme connecting canonical and noncanonical SL branches
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for formation of 4-oxo-carlactonoic acid and downstream 4-oxo-MeCLA in reconstructed pathway; also functions in canonical SL biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Os900 was strongly coexpressed with OsCYP706C2 and lies in the same rice SL biosynthetic region. 2. In Nicotiana benthamiana, OsCYP706C2 plus Os900 converted carlactone-pathway products to a further oxidized metabolite assigned as 4-oxo-carlactonoic acid. 3. Adding methyltransferases yielded 4-oxo-MeCLA, supporting Os900 as the intermediate oxidation enzyme. 4. Prior os900 mutant data cited in this paper support accumulation of 4-oxo-19-OH-carlactone and near loss of downstream products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression analysis; pathway reconstitution in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS parent-ion scan; integration with previously reported rice mutant metabolite data
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-expressed; induced by phosphate deficiency; highly similar expression profile to OsCYP706C2
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rice roots under phosphate deficiency; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rhizosphere signaling and symbiosis-related strigolactone output
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper highlights an additional previously uncharacterized activity of Os900 in the noncanonical branch. Trace 4-oxo-MeCLA in os900 mutants suggests a minor bypass by other MAX1 homolog(s).
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important shared engineering target for altering both canonical and noncanonical rice strigolactones, but manipulation may broadly affect SL composition
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Mutual-rank coexpression using Rice Expression Database

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant of noncanonical branch, not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables final methylation step to produce 4-oxo-MeCLA in pathway reconstruction assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOC_Os01g50480 was identified as a candidate methyltransferase in the rice SL biosynthetic cluster. 2. Coexpression and genomic clustering linked it with OsCYP706C2 and Os900. 3. In Nicotiana benthamiana, adding this gene to OsCYP706C2+Os900+carlactone pathway genes produced 4-oxo-MeCLA. 4. The gene was therefore named Os4OCLAMT1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression analysis; genomic clustering; transient pathway reconstitution in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS/MS detection of 4-oxo-MeCLA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-expressed; induced by phosphate deficiency but less tightly coexpressed than OsCYP706C2/Os900
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Earlier work cited in the paper showed rice methyltransferases can also convert CLA to MeCLA. Functional differentiation between the two rice copies remains unresolved.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential terminal-step engineering target to specifically modulate noncanonical SL formation

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant of noncanonical branch, not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables final methylation step to produce 4-oxo-MeCLA in pathway reconstruction assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOC_Os01g50610 is a close homolog of LOC_Os01g50480 in the same rice biosynthetic gene cluster. 2. In Nicotiana benthamiana, addition of this gene to OsCYP706C2+Os900+carlactone pathway genes yielded 4-oxo-MeCLA. 3. The gene was named Os4OCLAMT2. 4. Whether Os4OCLAMT1 and Os4OCLAMT2 have distinct in planta roles remains unresolved.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genomic clustering; transient pathway reconstitution in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS/MS detection of 4-oxo-MeCLA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-expressed; induced by phosphate deficiency but less tightly coexpressed than OsCYP706C2/Os900
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Earlier work cited in the paper showed rice methyltransferases can also convert CLA to MeCLA. Functional differentiation between the two rice copies remains unresolved.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential terminal-step engineering target to modulate noncanonical SL production, possibly with redundancy considerations
